element_introduction_order.element: Jim's robotic suit and basic movement
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic platforming controls immediately
Used as primary method of shooting enemies
Whipping enemies and grabbing/swinging from hooks; also used to control NPCs like Peter Puppy
Variety of themed bosses: Hickboy, Psy-Crow, Queen Slug-for-a-Butt, Evil the Cat, Bob the Killer Goldfish, Major Mucus, Professor Monkey-for-a-Head
Third-person perspective racing through tube-like structure; competitive against Psy-Crow; win/loss determines progression method
Must keep NPC alive by whipping over pits and defeating enemies; failure results in health depletion
Competitive variation requiring both vertical movement and combat
Navigation constraint with crash penalty system

typical_relief_triggers: Winning Andy Asteroids race (skip boss requirement)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycles of platforming exploration, specialized challenge variations, and boss climaxes; racing segments provide tonal shifts between traditional level structure

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (gun, whip, basic platforming) in controlled environments
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation through specialized level types (escort missions, competitive racing); boss variety increases in complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-consequence mechanics (underwater maze with crash penalty, time pressure, complex escort sequences)
notable_spikes: For Pete's Sake level (introduces dependent NPC protection mechanic)
Andy Asteroids races (introduces third-person perspective and competitive failure state)
Late-game specialized levels with compound constraints

notable_level_patterns.description: Horizontal traversal through obstacles with gun/whip mechanics, culminates in themed boss battle
Protects computer-controlled NPC (Peter Puppy) through level; requires whipping NPC over gaps and managing enemy threats to keep NPC alive
Third-person perspective racing segment between traditional levels; victory advances automatically, loss triggers mandatory boss fight
One-on-one competitive gameplay mixing bungee-jumping and fighting mechanics
Underwater maze requiring navigation within time limit and crash penalty system

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Earthworm Jim (SNES)
